heading: Cooperative agreement between Department of the Interior and Indian tribes
label: 3115 Cooperative agreement between Department of the Interior and Indian tribes

children.label: A
children.text: engage in cooperative manpower and job training and development programs,

children.label: B
children.text: to develop and publish cooperative environmental education and natural resource planning materials, and

children.label: C
children.text: to perform land and facility improvements, including forestry and other natural resources protection, fire protection, reforestation, timber stand improvement, debris removal, and other activities related to land and natural resource management.

In such cooperative agreements, the Secretary is authorized to advance or reimburse funds to contractors from any appropriated funds available for similar kinds of work or by furnishing or sharing materials, supplies, facilities or equipment without regard to the provisions of section 3324, title 31, relating to the advance of public moneys.

children.heading: Supervision
children.label: b Supervision
children.text: In any agreement authorized by this section, Indian tribes and their employees may perform cooperative work under the supervision of the Department of the Interior in emergencies or otherwise as mutually agreed to, but shall not be deemed to be Federal employees other than for purposes of section 2671 through 2680 of title 28 and section 8101 through 8193 of title 5.11So in original. Probably should be “sections”.1

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: c Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to limit the authority of the Secretary to enter into cooperative agreements otherwise authorized by law.
